title: 7 Herbal Tinctures Proven to Help Manage Gastritis Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for gastritis because it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, preventing irritation and discomfort.
Flavonoids help to reduce inflammation and fight free radicals in the stomach, which can help to heal and prevent gastritis, a condition characterized by stomach inflammation and irritation.

Saponins in Turmeric (Curcuma longa) are good for gastritis because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause the condition, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, also known as licorice root tincture, is often used to help people with gastritis. Gastritis is an inflammation of the stomach lining, which can cause pain, nausea, and vomiting. The tincture of Glycyrrhiza glabra is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the stomach. It also has a protective effect on the stomach lining, which can help prevent further irritation and damage. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gastritis. When taken as a tincture, it can be easily absorbed into the body, providing quick relief from gastritis symptoms. It is also believed to help heal the stomach lining and restore its natural protective barrier, which can help prevent future episodes of gastritis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tinctures that help with gastritis, you'll need dried Turmeric roots or Curcuma longa, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a glass jar with a lid, and a clean environment. Start by measuring one part of dried Turmeric roots and adding it to two parts of your chosen carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well to combine, then seal the jar with its lid and place it in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process allows the active compounds in the Turmeric roots to infuse into the carrier oil. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Store the resulting tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help soothe gastritis symptoms. It's recommended to take 5-10 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for gastritis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the stomach lining and protect it from acid damage.

Shogaols are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach lining, protecting it from irritation and helping to heal damaged tissues.
Curcumenol is good for gastritis because it helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and prevents the production of stomach acid, which can cause pain and damage to the stomach.

Polyphenols are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, allowing the digestive system to heal and recover from irritation and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, is a natural remedy for gastritis. The tincture form of ginger is easily absorbed by the body and can provide quick relief from stomach pain and inflammation. Ginger contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining. This can help to alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as heartburn, nausea, and vomiting. The tincture also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm the stomach and promote digestion. Additionally, ginger has natural antimicrobial properties, which can help to eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that may be contributing to gastritis. Regular use of ginger tincture may also help to prevent future episodes of gastritis by promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing inflammation in the stomach.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Curcuma longa herbal tincture that helps with gastritis, first, you'll need to gather the necessary materials. You'll need dried turmeric roots, vodka or a high-proof grain alcohol, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a coffee filter or cheesecloth. Start by measuring out one part of dried turmeric roots to two parts of vodka, then fill the glass jar about 2/3 full with the turmeric and pour the vodka over it, making sure the turmeric is completely covered. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbal properties infuse into the vodka. After the infusion time is up, strain the liquid through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place, using 20-30 drops as needed to help soothe gastritis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for gastritis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, and also protects the lining of the stomach from damage caused by acid.
Luteolin is good for gastritis because it reduces inflammation and heals the stomach lining by blocking the production of chemicals that cause pain and irritation.
Quercetin is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
Tannins are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, protecting it from acid damage and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help with gastritis. Gastritis is inflammation of the stomach lining, which can cause pain, nausea, and discomfort. The active compounds in peppermint, called menthol and menthone, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach lining. When taken as a tincture, peppermint can relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce spasms, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis. Additionally, peppermint has natural antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gastritis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, peppermint tinctures can help calm the stomach and provide relief from gastritis symptoms. When taken in moderation, peppermint tinctures can be a safe and effective way to manage gastritis symptoms and promote digestive health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture that helps with gastritis, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, gather 1 part of dried Curcuma longa root, which you can find at a local health food store or online. Then, combine it with 2 parts of a neutral-tasting liquid like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is airtight and label it with the date and contents. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the herbs release their medicinal properties into the liquid. After the steeping time is up, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops, mixed with water, to help soothe and calm the stomach and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal tinctures for gastritis

Coumarins are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, promoting healing and soothing the stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant-based remedy that can help ease symptoms of gastritis. The tincture, which is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, contains compounds that can calm inflammation in the stomach lining and reduce acidity. Fennel's natural anise flavor and aroma can also help to soothe digestive issues like bloating and cramps. When taken as a tincture, fennel's active compounds can be quickly absorbed by the body, providing fast relief from gastritis symptoms. Additionally, fennel tincture has been traditionally used to aid digestion, ease nausea, and promote healthy gut bacteria, making it a potential natural treatment for gastritis sufferers. Fennel's antispasmodic properties can also help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with gastritis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for gastritis relief, start by gathering the necessary materials: turmeric roots, a glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof alcohol, and a dark glass bottle for storage. Begin by cleaning and drying the turmeric roots, then chop them into small pieces and place them in the glass jar. Fill the jar with vodka, making sure the roots are completely covered. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the mixture steep. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. This tincture can be taken orally, typically 1/4 to 1 teaspoon, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or taken as is, to help alleviate gastritis symptoms and reduce inflammation.

Flavonoids, also known as turmeric, are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, thereby healing and protecting the stomach lining from damage.
Aloin is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach, and also protects the stomach lining from acid damage.

Saponins are good for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a liquid extract of the aloe vera plant. It's used to help heal and soothe the stomach lining. When you have gastritis, the stomach lining gets inflamed and irritated, causing pain and discomfort. The aloe vera tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. It also has a protective layer that shields the stomach from acid and digestive enzymes, giving it time to heal. This can help relieve symptoms of gastritis, such as heartburn, bloating, and stomach pain. Additionally, aloe vera has antioxidant properties that help repair damaged cells and promote healthy digestion. When taken in moderation, the tincture can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan, providing quick relief and promoting long-term stomach health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tincture, start by gathering dried turmeric roots. You can dry them yourself by spreading them on a paper towel and letting them sit in a warm, dry place for a few days. Next, combine 1 part dried turmeric roots with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is filled to the top, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process, called maceration, allows the alcohol to extract the active compounds from the turmeric roots. After the 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles, label them, and store them in a cool, dark place. The tincture can be taken as needed, usually 20-30 drops in water or tea, to help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for gastritis

Quinidine is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from acid damage.
Quinic acid is good for gastritis because it helps reduce inflammation in the stomach and promotes the healing of damaged tissues, relieving symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea.
Tannins are good for gastritis because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach lining, and also protect it from acid damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tincture is often used to help people with gastritis. This is because it contains compounds called quinine and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining, making it less painful to eat and drink. The tincture also has natural antibacterial properties, which can help kill the bacteria that can cause gastritis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Cinchona officinalis tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of gastritis, such as stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting. Additionally, the tincture may also help protect the stomach lining from further damage, promoting healing and preventing future episodes of gastritis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Curcuma longa herbal tincture that can help with gastritis, you will need a few simple ingredients and tools. Start by gathering 1 part of dried Curcuma longa root, 1 part of dried peppermint leaves, and 1 part of dried ginger root. Combine these ingredients in a clean glass jar, making sure they are completely covered with 80-proof vodka or a high-proof spirit. The ratio of herbs to liquid is important, as it allows the active compounds to infuse into the liquid. Next, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. This process, called maceration, allows the herbs to release their properties into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water or tea to help alleviate gastritis symptoms. Remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for gastritis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for gastritis because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach, allowing the lining to heal and recover faster.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing stomach ulcers and inflammation caused by gastritis.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin is good for gastritis because it has powerful anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach, and also help protect the stomach lining from damage and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tincture, also known as turmeric tincture, is a natural remedy that can help with gastritis. Gastritis is an inflammation of the stomach lining that can cause pain, nausea, and vomiting. Turmeric tincture contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has strong anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally, curcumin can help reduce the inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of gastritis. The tincture can also help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by digestive enzymes and acids, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage. By taking turmeric tincture, people with gastritis may experience relief from symptoms and improved digestive health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tinctures that help with gastritis, you'll need dried turmeric roots, a clean glass jar, vodka or glycerin as the solvent, and a dark glass bottle for storage. First, measure one part of dried turmeric roots to two parts of vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. For example, use 100g of turmeric roots and 200ml of vodka. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active compounds into the solvent. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and label it. This homemade Curcuma longa tincture can be taken sublingually or added to water to help soothe gastritis symptoms, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're pregnant or have any underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for gastritis

intro_desc: Gastritis is a condition where the stomach lining becomes inflamed, leading to pain, discomfort, and digestive issues. Using herbal tinctures can be beneficial in managing gastritis symptoms. One effective herb is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Another useful herb is Zingiber officinale, or ginger, which has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can also help calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea. These herbs can be combined in a tincture to provide relief from gastritis symptoms.  Fixing gastritis is crucial because it can lead to complications like ulcers, bleeding, and even scarring of the stomach lining. If left untreated, gastritis can significantly impact daily life, causing persistent pain, digestive issues, and fatigue. This can make everyday activities challenging, affecting work, social life, and relationships. By managing gastritis symptoms with herbal tinctures, individuals can improve their quality of life, reducing discomfort and enabling them to participate in activities they enjoy. This, in turn, can boost mood, energy levels, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best: For gastritis, combining the tinctures of 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (licorice root), 'Curcuma longa' (turmeric), and 'Mentha x piperita' (peppermint) can be beneficial.  Licorice root tincture helps to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and pain. Turmeric tincture contains curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach. Peppermint tincture can help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms that often accompany gastritis.  The combination of these three tinctures can work synergistically to address the various aspects of gastritis. The licorice root can protect the stomach lining, while the turmeric can reduce inflammation and promote healing. The peppermint can help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing pain and discomfort. This combination can provide comprehensive support for the stomach and can be taken in small amounts (about 10-20 drops of each tincture, 2-3 times a day) mixed with water or another liquid.
supplementary_avoid: If you have gastritis, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can irritate your stomach or make your symptoms worse. One such herb is 'Angelica archangelica' (Angelica), which can cause stomach upset and worsen gastritis due to its alkaloid content. Another herb to avoid is 'Echinacea purpurea' (Echinacea), as it can stimulate digestion and increase stomach acid, which may not be suitable for people with gastritis.  Additionally, 'Ephedra sinica' (Ephedra) is a herb to avoid as it can increase stomach acid production and cause stomach irritation.  Other herbs to be cautious with are 'Ginkgo biloba' and 'Panax ginseng', as they may not be suitable for everyone, especially in high doses, and could potentially irritate the stomach or interact with medications.  Herbs like 'Urtica dioica' (Nettle) and 'Taraxacum officinale' (Dandelion) may also not be the best choices, as they can stimulate digestion and increase urine production, which may not be suitable for people with gastritis.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice is often used to help soothe and calm the digestive system, which can be beneficial for people with gastritis. The gel inside the aloe plant contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach lining. Drinking aloe vera juice may help to reduce stomach pain and discomfort associated with gastritis.  Probiotics are also a natural remedy that can aid in digestion and help alleviate symptoms of gastritis. Probiotics are live bacteria and yeasts that are beneficial for the gut, and they can help restore the balance of good bacteria in the stomach. This can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it easier for the stomach to recover from gastritis.  Another natural remedy that can help with gastritis is slippery elm. The inner bark of the slippery elm tree contains mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that can help to protect and soothe the stomach lining. When taken as a tea or capsule, slippery elm may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach, making it a helpful natural remedy for people with gastritis.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with gastritis often experience other ailments like acid reflux, stomach ulcers, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). These conditions are connected to gastritis because they share similar root causes, such as inflammation and imbalance in the gut's natural bacteria. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera, marshmallow root, and licorice root can help heal these ailments. Aloe vera soothes the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and calming acid reflux. Marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the stomach lining, preventing acid from irritating it further. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the stomach. These herbs work by neutralizing stomach acid, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing in the stomach lining. For IBS, herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and dandelion root can help alleviate symptoms like bloating and abdominal pain. Peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, while chamomile tea calms the mind and body, reducing stress-related digestive issues. Dandelion root stimulates digestion and reduces bloating.  Some people with gastritis may also experience low stomach acid, which can be addressed with herbs like betaine HCl and pepsin. Betaine HCl stimulates the production of stomach acid, which helps break down food and reduce symptoms of gastritis. Pepsin is an enzyme that helps digest protein and reduces inflammation in the stomach. These herbs work by stimulating the digestive system and restoring balance to the gut's natural bacteria.
